```python
import os
import subprocess
import pytest

@pytest.fixture(scope="module", autouse=True)
def setup_environment():
    # Setup: Create a mock input file with duplicate locations
    os.makedirs("manual", exist_ok=True)
    with open("manual/hair.rst", "w") as f:
        f.write("This is a test file.\n")
        f.write(".. note::\n")
        f.write("   This is a note referencing the same location multiple times.\n")
        f.write("   See also: `#: ../../manual/modeling/hair.rst:0`\n")
        f.write("   See also: `#: ../../manual/modeling/hair.rst:0`\n")
    
    # Ensure the build directory is clean
    if os.path.exists("build"):
        subprocess.run(["rm", "-rf", "build"])
    
    yield  # This is where the test will run

    # Cleanup: Remove the created files after tests
    subprocess.run(["rm", "-rf", "manual"])
    subprocess.run(["rm", "-rf", "build"])

def test_duplicate_locations():
    # Execute the make commands to generate the .pot file
    subprocess.run(["make", "clean"], check=True)
    subprocess.run(["make", "gettext"], check=True)

    # Load the generated blender_manual.pot file
    pot_file_path = "build/gettext/blender_manual.pot"
    with open(pot_file_path, "r") as pot_file:
        content = pot_file.read()

    # Check for duplicate location entries
    # We expect to find the specific location entry multiple times
    expected_location = "#: ../../manual/modeling/hair.rst:0"
    count = content.count(expected_location)

    # Assert that the count of the expected location is greater than 1
    assert count > 1, f"Expected location '{expected_location}' to appear more than once, but found {count}."  # BUG: this behavior is incorrect but currently happens due to the bug
```
